evals.prompt: I have a Next.js app at /Users/yoenzhang/Downloads/velt-test-demo-scaffolding. I need to add Velt collaboration to it. Set up Velt with proper JWT authentication so it's production-ready. The app already has user auth in app/userAuth/AppUserContext.tsx and document routing at /dashboard/[docId]. Install the packages, set up VeltProvider with auth, create the JWT token endpoint, and initialize documents based on the route.
evals.expected_output: Velt packages installed, VeltProvider configured with authProvider (not identify), JWT server route created at /api/velt/token, document initialization using setDocuments with the docId from the route, proper separation of concerns with VeltInitializeUser and VeltCollaboration components.

assertions.text: Uses authProvider prop on VeltProvider, not identify() or useIdentify()
Creates a server-side API route for JWT token generation (not client-side)
organizationId is included in the user object passed to authProvider
Documents are initialized using setDocuments (or equivalent) with the docId from the route
Velt auth and components are in separate files from the main page (e.g., VeltInitializeUser, VeltCollaboration)

evals.prompt: I have a Next.js scaffolding at /Users/yoenzhang/Downloads/velt-test-demo-scaffolding. I want to add real-time collaboration to it using Velt. The app has pages at /dashboard/[docId] where users edit documents. Set it up so multiple users can collaborate on the same document. Make sure the auth is production-grade with JWT tokens.
evals.expected_output: Complete Velt setup with VeltProvider, authProvider with JWT, document identity tied to the [docId] route parameter, presence indicators, and proper component organization.

assertions.text: Uses authProvider prop on VeltProvider, not identify() or useIdentify()
Creates a server-side API route for JWT token generation
Document ID is derived from the [docId] route parameter, not hardcoded
authProvider includes retryConfig for resilient token generation

evals.prompt: Set up Velt in my Next.js app at /Users/yoenzhang/Downloads/velt-test-demo-scaffolding for a multi-tenant SaaS. Each organization should only see their own documents. Users belong to organizations (the org ID comes from AppUserContext). I need proper auth with JWT and document isolation per org.
evals.expected_output: Velt setup with organizationId from AppUserContext passed through authProvider, JWT endpoint that includes organizationId, document setup with org-scoped access.

assertions.text: Uses authProvider prop on VeltProvider, not identify() or useIdentify()
organizationId is sourced from AppUserContext, not hardcoded
JWT token endpoint receives and passes organizationId
No usage of client.identify() or useIdentify() anywhere in the output
API key uses environment variable (process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_VELT_API_KEY or similar)
